title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranked as the most collision-prone city in the U.S. for the second year, per Allstate's 2026 report.
Worcester and Springfield in Massachusetts also ranked low for driver safety, placing 197th and 196th respectively nationwide.
Allstate's report includes data from 2023-2024, analyzing collision trends and driver's phone use, influencing crash risks.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, led police on a wrong-way high-speed chase at 105 mph across the Bourne Bridge.
Deleon faces multiple charges including obstructing an emergency vehicle and assault with a dangerous weapon.
He was arrested after barricading himself at home and is held without bail; a pretrial hearing is set for Aug. 5.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released surveillance photos of a second juvenile involved in a South Boston lemonade stand robbery.
The first suspect, a 14-year-old, was charged with armed robbery after the June 10 incident on West Ninth Street.
Community rallied as the lemonade stand reopened; half of proceeds go to a gun violence prevention organization.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering his options for his final NBA team, with a focus on winning a fifth championship.
Rich Paul included the Boston Celtics as a possible, but less favored, destination for James on a podcast.
The Celtics' potential lineup with James includes Derrick White, Paul George, Mitchell Robinson, and Jayson Tatum.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticizes the Boston Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish compares the Brown trade to the controversial Luka Doncic trade by the Dallas Mavericks to the Lakers.
Parish believes Red Auerbach would have disapproved of the trade, but hopes for future amends between Brown and the Celtics.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum posted a farewell message to Jaylen Brown after his trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded for Paul George and four draft picks, including two first-round selections.
Brown expressed surprise and felt disrespected by the Celtics' handling of his trade process.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics and Neemias Queta agree to a four-year extension worth $56 million, extending his contract through 2030-31.
Queta, 26, posted 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks in 76 games during the 2025-26 NBA season.
The extension follows Jaylen Brown's trade to the 76ers and Mitchell Robinson's recent deal with the Celtics.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates its 200th anniversary as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The building, possibly dating back to 1716, housed historical figures and served diverse roles before becoming a restaurant in 1826.
Union Oyster House serves 1,500 guests daily, maintaining historic dishes like its famous clam chowder from the original 1826 menu.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's annual events include the July 4th Pops Spectacular with performances and fireworks at the Charles River Esplanade.
A World Cup quarterfinal will be held at Boston Stadium, formerly Gillette Stadium, on July 9 at 4 p.m.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail starts July 11, featuring over 60 tall ships marking America's 250th anniversary.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston has hosted six U.S. presidents, including John F. Kennedy and Barack Obama.
Famous guests range from actors like Al Pacino to athletes like Tiger Woods and Wayne Gretzky.
Artist Stan Kotzen created a caricature painting in 2003 featuring over 50 legendary restaurant visitors.
